reference: Hi. First of all, there cannot be a cancerous spot on the spleen. In a patient who is eighty-five years old, this can be due to an infarct or hemangioma, which can cause severe pain. Since you have provided a history related to her food habits and taste, it is possible she has other forms of gastrointestinal disease, which need thorough investigations. I would advise a computed tomography scan of the abdomen, an upper gastrointestinal endoscopy, and tests of blood, urine, and stool. I can understand the feelings you have about your mother. If there is no cancer, she can live much longer.
text: Hi. First of all, there cannot be a cancerous spot on the spleen. In a patient who is 85 years old, this can be due to an infarct or hemangioma, which can cause pain. Since you added a history related to her food habits and taste, it is possible she has other forms of gastrointestinal disease, which thorough investigations. I would advise a computed tomography scan of the abdomen and upper gastrointestinal endoscopy and tests of blood, urine and stool. I can understand the feelings you have about your mother. If there is no cancer, she can live much longer.
